steps.title: Find the growth factor per 4 minutes
steps.content:
We have N(m) = 72 Q^{m/8}. After 4 minutes, m changes to m+4.
$$\frac{N(m+4)}{N(m)} = \frac{72 Q^{(m+4)/8}}{72 Q^{m/8}} = Q^{(m+4)/8 - m/8} = Q^{4/8} = Q^{1/2}.$$
Thus the population multiplies by $Q^{1/2}$ every 240 seconds.

steps.title: Convert growth factor to percent increase
steps.content:
A p% increase means the new amount is $(1 + \frac{p}{100})$ times the original. Set this equal to the growth factor:
$$1 + \frac{p}{100} = Q^{1/2}.$$
Solve for p:
$$\frac{p}{100} = Q^{1/2} - 1 \quad \Longrightarrow \quad p = 100\left(Q^{1/2} - 1\right).$$
That is the correct answer.
